:mod:`The HTML class` 
=====================

.. module:: services.html

.. class:: HTML(object)

    A very lightweight class to help with HTML page rendering. It relies on an environment
    variable called **SEMPERPY_WEB** which is expected to have two subdirectories:
        
        * css containing cascading style sheet files (css)
        * templates containing .tmpl files as described further

    Some sample files are part of the distribution in the *web* directory.

Public methods
--------------

    .. staticmethod:: list(items):

        :param items: list of elements to render in an HTML list

        Returns an HMTL string using <ul> and <li> tags. List elements are the elements
        of the list *items*. The elements are expected to be strings or lists, if these 
        are lists, this method works recursively.

    .. classmethod:: webPath()

        Returns the contents of the **SEMPERPY_WEB** environmement variable always 
        ending with '/'. The server install a static page handler in that location, so
        any file (script, css, html etc...) will be served from there.

    .. classmethod:: substituteVariables(template_name,**kargs)

        :param template_name: name of the template to be used (**SEMPERPY_WEB**/templates/name.tmpl)
        :type template_name: string

        Substitutes keywords in the template files which are part of \*\*kargs and part of the 
        contents of the templare with their value, if *templates/body.tmpl* contains::

            <!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01 Transitional//EN">
            <html>
            <head>
            <title>__title__</title>
            <link rel="StyleSheet" href="css/style.css" type="text/css" />
            <meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=UTF-8"/>
            </head>

            <body>
            <h1>__welcome__</h1>
            __content__
            </body>

        The code::

            from semperpy.services.hmtl import HMTL

            print HTML.substituteVariables('body',welcome="Welcome to HTML",content="This is an example",title="example")

        produces:

            .. image:: template.tiff

    .. classmethod:: keywords(text)

        :param text: any text, more likeky the contents of a template file.
        :type text: string
        
        returns the list of keywords used in the strings (keywords are prefixed and suffixed with '__')::

            from semperpy.services.html import HTML

            print HTML.keywords("The __weather__ is nice __today__")
            ['weather','nice']

Private methods
---------------

    .. classmethod:: template(name)

        :param name: the name of the template file (without .tmpl)
        :type name: string

        Reads and returns the contents of a template file.
